page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the processor exhibits modest compute capabilities. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 10% of performance, with scores of 766.78 and 1533.17 respectively. Similarly, the PassMark CPU score of 5352.43 and physics test score of 499.76 fall into the bottom 25%. However, the CPU performs moderately in cryptographic operations, achieving an average PassMark encryption score of 4228.49, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

Memory performance is mixed. While the cached small-block read bandwidth reaches 200.22 GB/sec, large-block uncached reads and writes drop to 29.06 GB/sec and 15.26 GB/sec, which are categorized as weak. The PassMark memory score of 2075.12 sits in the middle 50%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show average performance, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput remain relatively low.

Given the high memory density relative to compute power, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense CPU processing. It is suited for caching layers, lightweight database operations, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that operate within a 2 Gbps network baseline.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 CPU cores running at 2.6 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates modest compute performance, with stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores falling in the lower percentiles. However, it maintains average performance in cryptographic tasks and single-threaded compression. Memory bandwidth is moderate, with cached reads reaching 200.22 GB/sec. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads. It is best suited for caching systems, in-memory databases, and general memory-intensive applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance in cryptographic and single-threaded compression tasks
Best suited for caching layers and memory-intensive applications
